revision.text:
{{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=42.98|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=49.14|label=Tamateyama
Kofun Cluster|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Tamateyama Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Tamateyama Kofun Cluster''' (玉手山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|lt=zenpokoenfun (keyhole-shaped tumuli)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} housing a house-shaped stone coffin. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A {{ill|zenpokoenfun|lt=zenpokoenfun (keyhole-shaped tumuli)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster|ja|高井田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Matsugatake-yama Kofun|ja|松岳山古墳|1=Matsuokayama Kofun|2=ja|3=松岳山古墳|4=fr|5=Kofun de Matsuokayama|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[en:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[sn:amateyama Kofun Group]]

File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster|ja|高井田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Matsugatake-yama Kofun|ja|松岳山古墳|1=Matsuokayama Kofun|2=ja|3=松岳山古墳|4=fr|5=Kofun de Matsuokayama|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[en:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[sn:amateyama Kofun Group]]

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=42.98|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=49.14|label=Tamateyama
Kofun Cluster|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Tamateyama Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Tamateyama Kofun Cluster''' (玉手山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato River|qq=}} and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|lt=zenpokoenfun (keyhole-shaped tumuli)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai region|qq=}}. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped stone coffin. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A {{ill|zenpokoenfun|lt=zenpokoenfun (keyhole-shaped tumuli)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster|ja|高井田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsugatake-yama Kofun|ja|松岳山古墳|1=Matsuokayama Kofun|2=ja|3=松岳山古墳|4=fr|5=Kofun de Matsuokayama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugatake-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuokayama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[en:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[sn:amateyama Kofun Group]]

File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster|ja|高井田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsugatake-yama Kofun|ja|松岳山古墳|1=Matsuokayama Kofun|2=ja|3=松岳山古墳|4=fr|5=Kofun de Matsuokayama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugatake-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuokayama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[en:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[sn:amateyama Kofun Group]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] → [[Takaida Cave Tombs]]
revision.text:
{{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=42.98|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=49.14|label=Tamateyama
Kofun Cluster|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Tamateyama Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Tamateyama Kofun Cluster''' (玉手山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato River|qq=}} and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|lt=zenpokoenfun (keyhole-shaped tumuli)|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai region|qq=}}. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped stone coffin. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A {{ill|zenpokoenfun|lt=zenpokoenfun (keyhole-shaped tumuli)|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|lt=Takaida Cave Tombs|lt=Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster|ja|高井田横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsugatake-yama Kofun|ja|松岳山古墳|1=Matsuokayama Kofun|2=ja|3=松岳山古墳|4=fr|5=Kofun de Matsuokayama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugatake-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuokayama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[en:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[sn:amateyama Kofun Group]]

The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped stone coffin. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[en:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[sn:amateyama Kofun Group]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]]

One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[sn:amateyama Kofun Group]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}}

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Kofun of Osaka prefecture
revision.text:
{{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=42.98|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=49.14|label=Tamateyama
Kofun Cluster|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Tamateyama Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Tamateyama Kofun Cluster''' (玉手山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped stone coffin. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[sn:amateyama Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture�]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}}

Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[sn:amateyama Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Fixed Kofun templates
revision.text:
{{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=42.98|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=49.14|label=Tamateyama
Kofun Cluster|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Tamateyama Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Tamateyama Kofun Cluster''' (玉手山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped stone coffin. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[sn:amateyama Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}}

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583}}; {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.56193888888889|N|135.63031666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11572620]] Wikidata name: Tamateyama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=42.98|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=49.14|label=Tamateyama
Kofun Cluster|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Tamateyama Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Tamateyama Kofun Cluster''' (玉手山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped stone coffin. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]]}} [[sn:Tamateyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[sn:amateyama Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}}

Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped stone coffin. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}}

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583}}; {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.56193888888889|N|135.63031666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11572620]] Wikidata name: Tamateyama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=42.98|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=49.14|label=Tamateyama
Kofun Cluster|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Tamateyama Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Tamateyama Kofun Cluster''' (玉手山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped Sarcophagus. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}}

Kofun Cluster|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Tamateyama Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Tamateyama Kofun Cluster''' (玉手山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped Sarcophagus. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}}

Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583}}; {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.56193888888889|N|135.63031666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572620]] Wikidata name: Tamateyama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=42.98|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=49.14|label=Tamateyama
Kofun Cluster|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Tamateyama Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Tamateyama Kofun Cluster''' (玉手山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped Sarcophagus. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
