'''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped Sarcophagus. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583}}; {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.56193888888889|N|135.63031666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572620]] Wikidata name: Tamateyama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=42.98|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=49.14|label=Tamateyama
Kofun Cluster|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Tamateyama Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Tamateyama Kofun Cluster''' (玉手山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped Sarcophagus. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]]. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped Sarcophagus. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * [[:en:Takaida Cave Tombs|Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster]] * [[:en:Matsuokayama Kofun|Matsuokayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with a total length of 107 meters. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|Takaida Cave Tombs|WD=Q11668714|lt=Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster}} * {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|en|Matsuokayama Kofun|WD=Q11530088|lt=Matsuokayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583}}; {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.56193888888889|N|135.63031666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572620]] Wikidata name: Tamateyama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=42.98|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=49.14|label=Tamateyama
Kofun Cluster|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Tamateyama Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Tamateyama Kofun Cluster''' (玉手山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}} and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|WD=Q906708|lt=Kinai region}}. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped Sarcophagus. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|Takaida Cave Tombs|WD=Q11668714|lt=Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster}} * {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|en|Matsuokayama Kofun|WD=Q11530088|lt=Matsuokayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583}}; {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.56193888888889|N|135.63031666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=42.98|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=49.14|label=Tamateyama
Kofun Cluster|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Tamateyama Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Tamateyama Kofun Cluster''' (玉手山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}} and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|WD=Q906708|lt=Kinai region}}. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped Sarcophagus. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * {{ill|Takaida Yokoanabos|en|Takaida Yokoanabos|WD=Q11668714|lt=Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster}} * {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|en|Matsuokayama Kofun|WD=Q11530088|lt=Matsuokayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|WD=Q906708|lt=Kinai region|1=Kinai}}. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped Sarcophagus. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * {{ill|Takaida Yokoanabos|en|Takaida Yokoanabos|WD=Q11668714|lt=Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster|1=Takaida Yokoanabos}} * {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|en|Matsuokayama Kofun|WD=Q11530088|lt=Matsuokayama Kofun|1=Matsuokayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}; {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.56193888888889|N|135.63031666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=42.98|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=49.14|label=Tamateyama
Kofun Cluster|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Tamateyama Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Tamateyama Kofun Cluster''' (玉手山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara, Osaka|1=Kashiwara}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}} and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|WD=Q906708|lt=Kinai region|1=Kinai}}. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped Sarcophagus. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * {{ill|Takaida Yokoanabos|en|Takaida Yokoanabos|WD=Q11668714|lt=Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster|1=Takaida Yokoanabos}} * {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|en|Matsuokayama Kofun|WD=Q11530088|lt=Matsuokayama Kofun|1=Matsuokayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}} [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Zenpokoenfun" to "Zenpōkōenfun"
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Tamateyama Kofun Group]][[ja:玉手山古墳群]] [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}; {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.56193888888889|N|135.63031666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=42.98|lon_deg=135|lon_min=37|lon_sec=49.14|label=Tamateyama
Kofun Cluster|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Tamateyama Kofun Cluster}}}} '''Tamateyama Kofun Cluster''' (玉手山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara, Osaka|1=Kashiwara}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. The cluster is scattered along the alluvial hill of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|柏原市立玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|qq=}}, stretching north-south southeast of the confluence of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}} and the Ishikawa River. == Overview == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster consists of three groups of tumuli: 1. '''Early Kofun Cluster''': This consists of 13 {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} and over 20 round Kofuns (円墳). These tumuli are distributed across the entire hill, with burial facilities including {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, beads, swords, and tools were placed as burial items. On the mid-slope of the hill, a split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus with straight arc patterns, excavated from the cluster, has been repurposed as a washbasin at {{ill|Anpuku-ji Temple|ja|安福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anpuku-ji Temple|qq=}}. Large clusters of early kofun like this are exceedingly rare even in the {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|WD=Q906708|lt=Kinai region|1=Kinai}}. 2. '''Later Horizontal Chamber Tombs''': Near the western base of the hill, horizontal chamber tombs (横穴墓) have been carved into the mountainside, taking advantage of a tuff stratum. Though the exact number is unknown, it is estimated that there are dozens of such tombs. Sarcophagi, both carved and ceramic (陶棺), were used, and artifacts from the later period have been discovered. 3. '''Late Kofun''': This group includes a single late-period tumulus with a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a house-shaped Sarcophagus. Thus, although collectively called the Tamateyama Kofun Cluster, the first group significantly differs in nature from the second and third groups. == Tumuli List == === No. 1 Kofun (Komatsuyama Kofun) === A {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} with a total length of 107 meters. Evidence suggests the presence of a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the round mound's apex and a clay sarcophagus in the frontal part. A cylindrical haniwa coffin was excavated near the round mound's base. Designated as a cultural property (historic site) by Kashiwara City. === No. 2 Kofun === A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters. Investigations have not been conducted as the entire mound has been used as a cemetery. Dispersed slab stones suggest the existence of a stone chamber. === No. 3 Kofun (Shōbuyama Kofun) === Despite damage to the front part, this keyhole-shaped tumulus is reconstructed as over 95 meters in length. Radar exploration confirmed the presence of a stone chamber near the center of the round mound. === No. 4 Kofun === An estimated keyhole-shaped tumulus 50-60 meters long. Destroyed during development but excavated in 1960. The pit-style sarcophagus was rectangular with rounded corners and measured 6 meters in length and 3 meters in width. === No. 5 Kofun (Kagamihari Kofun) === A 75-meter-long keyhole-shaped tumulus. Excavated urgently due to development; no longer extant. The central part contained a pit-style sarcophagus and yielded numerous relics. File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-2.jpg|No. 1 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|No. 2 Kofun File:Tamateyama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|No. 3 Kofun == Construction Period == The Tamateyama Kofun Cluster is now considered to date back to the early first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, with activity ceasing by the mid-period. == Related Cultural Properties == ; Stone Coffin Lid at Anpuku-ji A split-bamboo-shaped sarcophagus lid with straight arc patterns located on the temple grounds. Designated an Important Cultural Property in 1990. ; Anpuku-ji Horizontal Tomb Cluster Over 35 horizontal tombs are scattered along the temple's pathway. One contains carved images of equestrian figures. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}} * {{ill|Takaida Yokoanabos|en|Takaida Yokoanabos|WD=Q11668714|lt=Takaida Horizontal Tomb Cluster|1=Takaida Yokoanabos}} * {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|en|Matsuokayama Kofun|WD=Q11530088|lt=Matsuokayama Kofun|1=Matsuokayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Kashiwara City Tamateyama Kofun Cluster] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|玉手山古墳群|version=98321838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:玉手山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Tamateyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11572620}} [[Category:History of Kashiwara, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
